architecture: Runtime Governance, Operational Admissibility, TrustMap, Adoption Strategy, Pilot Packaging, and Website Integration Prototype
core_thesis: AI is controlled before it acts, or it is not controlled at all.
core_question: Should this action be operationally admissible right now?

release_train.v21: Executive Demo QA and Navigation Compression
release_train.v26: Persistence, Intake, and CRM Payload Hardening

v28_capabilities: guided demo mode with Runtime, TrustMap, Reports, Adoption, Pilot sequence
markdown, text, and JSON report export
website integration modes and MJC copy blocks
v29_input_needed: Google Sheets structure and Apps Script decision
Microsoft 365 systems to include or exclude
critical_ux_rule: Every visible executive object that appears as a chip, metric, card, gate, node, report, status, or control must either explain itself on hover/focus or navigate somewhere useful. No dead bubbles.
critical_architecture_rule: CyberShield must remain consolidated and performant. Do not reintroduce old phase-file module sprawl, broad MutationObserver loops, dynamic script injection, or timestamp-based runtime loading.
important_boundaries: The current public build is a lightweight static prototype.
The current public build is not connected to live enterprise telemetry.
The current public build is not connected to Google Sheets, Microsoft 365, or a real CRM.
The current public build is not a production SIEM, EDR, GRC, identity, cloud control, CRM, or agent enforcement platform.
CyberShield should not be represented as fully deployed live runtime enforcement until integrations exist.
CyberShield shows decision rationale traces, not private hidden chain-of-thought.
commercial_entry_wedge: CyberShield Readiness Review or Operational Trust Assessment led by Maximum Justice Cybersecurity and Dr. Max Justice, vCISO, Security SME, Cybersecurity SME, U.S. veteran, and creator of The CHN vCISO GPT powered by Cyber Shield.
